purchase_history.description: Award-winning, revered singer/songwriter Lucinda Williams is working to help independent music venues during this time with the announcement of Lu’s Jukebox. Scheduled as a six-episode series of mostl
purchase_history.review_text: As a rule I tend to really enjoy covers discs.  Not this go 'round.  Does neither Tom Petty nor Lucinda Williams justice. purchase_history.review_text: Another Chicago retrospective, this time an import.  Trouble is the same as ever.  The Columbia recordings far outshine the Warners years, leaving their '80s output seeming incredibly limp and middle-of-the-road. purchase_history.title: lost in the wilderness
purchase_history.review_text: Eddie and David Brigati's lost masterpiece.  Why hasn't this classic Rascals side-bar story been reissued on CD?  It's easily the equal of "Felix Cavaliere" / "Destiny," and far outshines "Treasure." That something as good as this remains unattainable in today's music marketplace is positively mindb

purchase_history.title: Waves by ERIC ANDERSEN (2005-10-18)
purchase_history.review_text: The follow-up to his "The Street Was Always There" collection, and basically, it's more of the same. purchase_history.title: Live in Tokyo-1983
purchase_history.review_text: What would the Band be like without Robbie Robertson?  People wanted to know back in the early '80s. purchase_history.review_text: Daylight Again began as a duo project for Stephen Stills and Graham Nash, as David Crosby was in the throes of his legendary and debilitating drug addiction. purchase_history.title: Complete Clapton

purchase_history.review_text: Import version, basically the same as the domestic release but featuring a better cover (imho). And in that sense, it's all good, I suppose, although this material has been collected and re-collected numerous times.

purchase_history.review_text: This one is rare and the advertisers like to say rare but this one is. Glad I paid up to buy this one.

purchase_history.title: Star Lounge Collection - 98.7 FM - 1998
purchase_history.review_text: I always enjoyed hearing of them on the radio. I bought them when I could and a few disappeared somewhere so I always replace them, even when they have been ripped and saved for me. Every one of them is as good as you can get.

purchase_history.title: Star Lounge 2000 Collection
purchase_history.review_text: If you enjoy live music it doesn't get much better tun this. Just get it and enjoy it. I own pretty much all of them.

purchase_history.title: Sounds Of The Seventies - 1971

purchase_history.review_text: I was raised with many of these songs and this is an easy way to get most of the items I enjoy. Like all compilation discs, all the songs aren't my favorites. They are all very good though. Who love everything on the radio back then anyway?

purchase_history.title: Mixclusives Volume 4 Mtmx 101.9 (Rare Acoustic Songs)

purchase_history.review_text: I did not grow up in Chi town so I need new of tis station. I really enjoy the tunes and they are basically unpublished items. If you like 'em, get them when you can find them. They are not easy to find.

purchase_history.review_text: The disc is fine but as what happens many times, the plastic case is quite damaged. Not a big problem, but for what I pay for this disc, a little disappointed.

purchase_history.title: 99X Live X V: Shimmer and Shine
purchase_history.review_text: "She Talks To Angels" by The Black Crowes make it worth every penny. That and Alanis Morrisette make it darn good.

purchase_history.review_text: Tough to beat all these Star Lounge CD's Truly one-of-a-kind items.

purchase_history.title: Svetlanov Conducts Wagner / USSR Academic Symphony Orchestra (Melodiya)
purchase_history.description: Tannheister Overture, Lohengrin Prelude to Act 1, Prelude to Act III, Tristan und Isolde: Prelude & Liebestod, Die Walkure: Ride of the Valkyries, Siegfried Forest Murmurs, Die Meistersinger Overture,
purchase_history.review_text:
I expected very heavy, bombastic sort of interpretation of Wagner's orchestral music from Svetlanov, but these are surprisingly well-crafted and vivid performances.

Tannhäuser Overture( 14'06) starts very gently and biulds up to exhilarating climax with inner string part accentuated.

purchase_history.review_text:
This is no doubt one of the most unforgettable performances of 3rd, which surpasses other great recordings by Abbado, Horenstein, Bernstein, Levine, Haitink and Kubelik. Probably Bertini's finest recording in his entire Mahler cycle.

purchase_history.title: Mahler: Symphony No.5
purchase_history.review_text: James Levine is a hugely under-rated Mahlerian. purchase_history.review_text: I've always loved Kyung Wha Chung/Solti and Kennedy/Rattle version of Elgar's Violin Concerto for its depth of feeling and the sheer power of the musicianship that, I thought, is unrivalled by other violinists/Conductors, until I stumbled on this amazing performance by David Oistrakh's son Igor.<br

HOWEVER.

purchase_history.title: Music of Dido
purchase_history.review_text: This is some sort of tribute album and not really Dido, which you would know if Amazon had audio samples.  It's also ridiculously expensive.  I'd skip it.

purchase_history.title: Totally '80s
purchase_history.review_text: This album is well named because each and every song was a huge hit in the 80's.  You won't find any unfamiliar filler songs here.

purchase_history.title: Instrumental Tribute to Lady Gaga
purchase_history.review_text: This music doesn't sound the same as Lady Gaga's.  It's more electronic and has less punch and energy to it.  It would be OK if your pre-teen just wanted to sing along for practice or something, but I wouldn't buy it to listen to for entertainment.

purchase_history.title: Sounds of the Season the Country Collection
purchase_history.description: Sounds Of The Season The Country Collection
purchase_history.review_text: There are Christian artists on here along with well known country ones, and most of these songs are unfamiliar or at least not popular versions.  It's not a bad CD, but the title is misleading.

purchase_history.review_text: This collection of love songs is mainly from the 70's and 80's, and the songs are all familiar ones to me.  I didn't think they all matched the theme of the compilation but still liked them.

purchase_history.review_text: This has a lot of songs so it's a great value. The songs are popular ones but are done in a softer way. The pan pipes are there but aren't obnoxious. There is other instrumentation that helps bring them out.
